comments: For the first time, the Friday Night Connection has four tracks.
Almost the complete show, only TV's back-announce of the Boyzz track and show outro are missing.

sessions.details: Recorded 1974-10-31. First TX Top Gear, 1974-11-14 (Peel Session #3). No known commercial release.
Recorded 1979-02-19. First TX Peel Show, 1979-02-26 (Peel Session #3). No known commercial release.

track_listing.track: 'I'm The One That's Leaving (LP-Strange Man, Changed Man)' (Radar)
(TV trails Trevor Dann's revival of Rock On)

track_listing.details: LP-Carlos Santana & Buddy Miles! Live!; CBS
LP-Oneness, Silver Dreams - Golden Reality; CBS
7"; Zoom; tape flip in the middle of this

track_listing.track: Many Are Called, But Few Get Up

transcript.text: Hi there, this is Tommy Vance. Welcome to the Friday Rock Show. Tonight on the show, two sessions. From 1974, that Welsh music mafia band called Man. And we have the latest session for Radio 1 by The Skids.

We've got classic cuts from David Bowie, Carlos Santana and Buddy Miles among others. New tracks by Ashro, The Scorpions, Simple Minds and Van Gelis. Also, the Friday Night Connection and tonight for the first time, four tracks that connect.
But tonight, we start with Golden Earring.
Radio played that forgotten song.
This man sang, he has seen some.
Bram Tchaikovsky in a track from their new album, which is called Strange Man, Changed Man.
The track was called I'm the One That's Leaving, and it's recently come out on Radar.
And I'm playing it for a load of people who are doing a polytechnic associateship in mineral surveying.
Up, I think, at Nelson Hall, High Melton College, High Melton in Doncaster.
I've got to read all these names out now.
Jerry, Steve K, Steve D, Paul Murphy, Barmy, or possibly Barry, or possibly both.
Ian Laws, John M, John D, Kemp, Hugh, Static, Nick, Mike, Glenn, Phil,
Neil, Martin, Jed, Roderick, Mark, and the guy that wrote the letter,
Geraint King, better known as Taff.
And before that, Golden Earring, a track from an album that came out in 1973,
Radar Love, and we're playing it because John Holbrook,
of 38 Mall Drive Coventry, CB35VU, said,
how about playing it?
Right, on to our classic session from Man.
This was recorded 31st of October, 1974, and it stands up.
That's going to be released as a single, and it's by the Scorpions, who really are a great act.
It's a track also from the new album, which is coming out on Harvest very shortly.
It's called Love Drive.
And the cut is entitled, Is There Anybody There?
There's going to be a slight remix, I'm told, when it's put out as a single.
It's one of the best records I think I've heard in years.
And before that, another German band by the name of Asherah
and a track from their new album, which is called Correlations.
It's out on Virgin. The track was Fantasus.
And then from 1974, you heard Man and the track Day and Night
from our classic session.
The line-up, and let's face it,
they had quite a number of different line-ups during their career
that started in 1968.
In fact, 12 of them.
The line-up in 1974 was Mickie Jones on guitar,
Deke Leonard, keyboards and guitar,
Ken Whaley on bass,
and Terry Williams, the evil fella with whom I've supped the odd drink on drums.
The Friday Rock Show.
It's now 10.26 and here are the skits.
Sex Pestles and Silly Thing.
Which of course is their latest single.
It's also on the double LP, The Great Rock and Roll Swindle,
which is out at the moment on Virgin.
And before that, something from our session by the skits.
a different version of the Lou Reed song
Walk on the Wild Side.
I'd like to tell you here about something that's going to happen
a week tomorrow, not tomorrow Saturday
but the Saturday following. The return of a
program by the name of Rock On is going to
have a different time slot. It'll be 90
minutes in length from 4 o'clock
So that's the return of Rock On and it's really
going to be a very contemporary program.
A week tomorrow between
4 and 5.30 on Radio
One. I'm going to be working on the
show which i'm very happy about obviously i'm going to be working with somebody who's well
known in the nottingham area his name is trevor dan he will be the producer of the program and
as everybody in that area knows he's a man well into rock because for some considerable number
of years he hosted the rock show up there so do listen up for rock on starts a week tomorrow
on radio one between four and five thirty settle back for a nice nice segue
around listening to that man for years and i still can't work out how he does it i really can't
that's carlos santana and a track from the album diva dip oneness which is out on cbs it was called
song for diva dip it was for tina winder with lots and lots of best wishes from derrick and lynn and
also because i happen to know that she likes carlos santana i'd like to dedicate that record to sue
one of the nicest people i've ever met also one of the nicest people i've ever met somebody called
toe before that you heard carlos santana and buddy miles as they recorded together in 1972
from the live album the track was them changes we played it for arfun griffiths i hope i said
that right arfun of number eight tivoli street in cheltenham who wants me to say hi how are you
to all his friends prior to that rock and roll suicide david bowie live the nick hodgson of 16
buttermere road bradford and west yorks you'd like us to dig into the archives and find something
especially for you well obviously you've got to let us know just right to the friday rock show
radio one london w1a4ww my name is tommy vance and myself and mr wilson will hit the archives
Simple Minds and Life in a Day, which is a single on the Zoom label.
Before that, something from the latest session by the Skids,
who I see are doing nicely in the top 20 at number 13 with Into the Valley.
The track was called Zit, and I played it because Mum sent me a card,
and she said, please say hi to Tony and Jim, currently having a holiday.
Not really. They're in Stafford Jail.
They listen regularly. Get out soon, fellas, and don't go back.
Friday Rock Show, and it's just past 11 o'clock.
Back to our session from 1974 by Man.
This track's called A Hard Way to Die.
That's Vangelis, and a track from the album which is called China,
which is his latest album on Polydor.
And the track was called Chang Kuo,
which I believe is the way the Chinese say China.
And before that, you heard a track from 1974 from our session by Man.
It was called A Hard Way to Die,
and we played it because loads of people wanted to hear that band again.
great band they were in their time
including these people, Tony Ball and his wife
at 43 School Road, Frampton
Cottrell down there in Bristol
and Billy Hartley of Merton in County Durham
and that means time
for the Friday Night Connection
we had one incorrect
answer, there's no fooling you
but the one we chose is the one
by Gary Smith, who's all of 16 years of age
at 29 White Road, Chatham, Kent
Gary Smith, you get yourself a £10
record voucher you lucky fella
Well, I can get the money out of Tony Wilson, as always.
The answer was Moon.
Because the tracks that we played were Luna Sea, by Camel, from Moon Madness,
Money, by The Floyd, from Dark Side of the Moon,
and She's Not There by Santana, from Moonflower.
Mr. Smith, congratulations.
As you know, part of the deal is to put together your own Friday night connection.
Normally three pieces of music that have a theme or some sort of connection.
tonight we've got four pieces of music because that's the way that carrie and rather gary smith
wanted it now i'll tell you that there's a clue in the word four four tracks together with a theme
or if you like a friday night connection the friday night connection put together by gary
smith of 29 white road chatham in kent if you think you know what the connection is and i trust
that you've worked it out put it down on a postcard letter anything you like back of an

but don't forget the idea is to put three pieces of music together that have a connection and if
yours is the first out of the bag well the first out of the bag with the nicest connection and of
course the correct answer will send you a 10 pound record voucher and do it please because that way
we know the type of music that you like to hear and it's nice to hear your choice not just the
This jock is Joyce.
Address again, Friday Night Connection,
Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1,
London W1A 4WW.
Hello there, this is Dev Bevan of Electric Light Orchestra.
And I'll be on Star Special this coming Sunday,
playing around 30 tracks,
two hours of what I consider to be the best in music.
And I just hope you can join me.
This Sunday, Star Special.
Dev Bevan's Star Special, 7 o'clock on Sunday.
Friday night, the Friday Rock Show,
and here's our final offering from the Skids.
It's UK, and a track from their album Danger Money,
which is out on Polydor.
The track was called Nothing To Lose,
and before that, our final offering from the Skids,
which was called Hang On To The Shadows.
Right, here's a real tour de force,
recorded back in 1974,
10 minutes and 45 seconds long,
and fabulous.
It's man.
Many are called, but few get up.
That was Man, and many a call, but few get up.
The culmination of our classic session tonight, recorded in 1974.
Nice to hear that band again.
And we played it because there were a lot of man freaks around the place,
and I don't mean you ladies.
Tim Jones of 11 Wellmeadow Drive, Copthorne, Shrewsbury,
wrote me many, many letters saying, dig some man out.
Though he didn't actually know there was a Radio 1 session.
Well, Tim, now you do.
Also, so did Rod Laird of 35, Cottam Road, Cottam, Bristol.
He wanted to hear man as well.
The Boys and Back to Kansas.
